ZS-Pre.article_title: AI lessons from the boardroom: Daan Debie on how NS brings AI from pilot to practice
ZS-Pre.post:
How do you move AI from promising pilot to daily practice on the ground? 🚆

At NS, director of data, innovation, and AI, Daan Debie, shared how they are doing exactly that. Think real systems in production, like crowdedness prediction for trains and bike-availability estimates that help travelers plan their journeys.

What stood out to us:

✅ Strong governance
NS treats AI as critical infrastructure. Clear ownership, safeguards around data and privacy, and alignment with the EU AI Act are all part of the design, not an afterthought.

✅ Training across the organization
Engineers, product teams, and front-line staff learn how AI works, where it helps, and where human judgment stays in the lead.

✅ Users involved from day one
Travelers and NS colleagues help shape the tools, which leads to higher trust and adoption.

At KickstartAI, we see NS as an example of how Dutch organizations can implement AI responsibly, at scale, and in line with European values.

If you are working on AI in operations, ask: "Who owns this, who understands this, and who helped build this?" The answers will often determine success.

Read more about NS and Daan Debie’s approach on our website. 👇

#AIadoption #ResponsibleAI #Netherlands #PublicTransport #KickstartAI

ZS-Pre.article_title: Agentic Engineering II: Best practices to avoid slop and actually improve productivity with coding agents
ZS-Pre.post:
🤖 Coding agents can generate entire features in minutes. The real question is: how do you stay in control of what they produce?

In our new KickstartAI article, we share how teams can use autonomous coding agents without sacrificing quality or accountability.

The key idea: treat the agent like a very fast junior developer.

You set the goals. You define the tests. You review the code.

A few practices we highlight:
• Start with a small, well-scoped task instead of a full roadmap.
• Use tests as the “contract” between your intent and the agent’s output.
• Keep a tight feedback loop, refining prompts and context based on results.
• Log decisions so you can explain how the code came to be, which is vital under the EU AI Act.

For the Dutch ecosystem, this approach supports both innovation and responsibility. Teams gain speed and experimentation power, while still meeting high standards for reliability, safety, and transparency.

That is how we see AI adoption accelerating: not by skipping safeguards, but by designing them into the way we use tools like coding agents.

Read the full article here: https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-best-practices-coding-agents-avoid-slop-increase-productivity

#AI #engineering #softwarequality #KickstartAI #AIsafety

ZS-Pre.article_title: Agentic Engineering I: A non-technical guide to working with coding agents
ZS-Pre.post:
🚀 Coding agents promise speed, but real value comes from how you integrate them into your way of working.

At KickstartAI, our stance is clear: use coding agents to amplify people, not to bypass them.

If you are a product leader or engineering manager, here are practical starting points:

• Start small: Give agents contained, low-risk tasks like writing tests or refactoring.
• Make specs explicit: Clear requirements and acceptance criteria help agents stay on scope.
• Raise the bar on review: Treat agent output like a pull request from a new colleague.
• Watch maintenance: Track how agent-generated code behaves over time, not just on day one.

The big shift is mindset. Developers spend less time writing every line, and more time shaping the system, guiding the agent, and protecting quality.

For the Dutch ecosystem, this is a chance to combine high productivity with high standards, in line with the EU AI Act and our broader ambition to use AI for societal good.

We captured these insights in a concise, non-technical guide to agentic engineering.

👉 Read the full article: https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-non-technical-guide-working-with-coding-agents

#AgenticAI #softwareengineering #AIproductivity #KickstartAI

ZS-Pre.article_title: KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it
ZS-Pre.post:
✈️ How do you move from AI experiments to AI you can trust in daily operations?

CIO of KLM, Aart Slagt, shared a clear answer: an AI model only has value if people understand it and trust it.

At KLM, that shift is already visible:

✅ A catering prediction model helps load the right amount of meals, improving service quality and cutting food waste.
✅ A contingency fuel model supports smarter fueling decisions, with safety and efficiency hand in hand.
✅ “Talia,” KLM’s secure AI environment, gives employees a safe place to explore and apply AI.

What stands out is not just the tech, but the mindset. KLM treats AI as a partner for planning and optimization, while protecting what matters most: human contact in the cabin.

At KickstartAI, we strongly support this approach. The Dutch ecosystem advances fastest when:

• Teams understand how models work.
• Employees trust AI enough to use it in critical workflows.
• Governance and security are built in from day one.

We see many Dutch organizations stuck in “AI lab mode.” KLM shows what it looks like to move into focused, responsible deployment.

Curious to dive deeper into KLM’s journey and Aart Slagt’s lessons for other leaders? Read the full story on our site.

#AIadoption #ResponsibleAI #AviationInnovation #KickstartAI #Netherlands

ZS-Pre.article_title: Why managing GenAI projects requires a playbook tweak
ZS-Pre.post:
What does responsible GenAI look like in a high-stakes sector like finance? For us, it looks like partnership.

Together with ING, we co-created a GenAI financial health assistant that helps customers navigate complex questions about their money. The goal: better financial decisions, in a way that fits the EU AI Act and builds trust.

A few principles that shaped the collaboration 👇

⚖️ Regulation as a design input
We treated the EU AI Act as a starting point, not an afterthought. That meant clearer guardrails on what the assistant can say and how it explains itself.

🤝 Multi-disciplinary, iterative build
Product owners, domain experts, engineers, and legal teams worked in short loops. That rhythm helped us adapt quickly as we saw real user behavior.

📏 Scalable evaluation
We designed for continuous testing, monitoring, and improvement so the assistant can evolve safely over time.

For KickstartAI, this is what accelerating AI adoption in the Netherlands looks like: practical solutions, built responsibly, with impact at scale.

Read more about the ING collaboration and our lessons learned: https://kickstart.ai/news/why-managing-genai-projects-requires-playbook-tweak

#AIAct #ResponsibleAI #GenAI #Netherlands #FinancialServices

FS-Pre.article_title: AI lessons from the boardroom: Daan Debie on how NS brings AI from pilot to practice

How do you move AI from a promising pilot to a trusted part of daily operations? 🚆

At NS, director of data, innovation, and AI, Daan Debie, shared how they are doing exactly that. Think about AI systems that predict train crowdedness or estimate bike availability. Useful, yes, but the real story is how NS makes these solutions work in practice.

The key ingredients Daan highlighted:

🔹 Strong governance, so AI is aligned with strategy, safety, and regulation.
🔹 Training for staff at every level, so people understand, trust, and use AI.
🔹 User involvement from day one, so solutions solve real problems, not hypothetical ones.

This is the kind of approach we love to see at KickstartAI. It shows how AI can support operational decisions at scale, while respecting European values on privacy and responsibility.

If you are in a large organization struggling to scale beyond pilots, look closely at examples like NS. The lesson is clear: invest in people, process, and governance as much as in models.

At KickstartAI, we are proud to collaborate with partners that turn AI into real, everyday impact for travelers, employees, and society.

FS-Pre.article_title: Agentic Engineering II: Best practices to avoid slop and actually improve productivity with coding agents
FS-Pre.post:
Many teams try coding agents for quick wins. The real value appears when you connect them to long-term software quality and maintainability. 🔍✨

In our latest blog, we explore how to integrate coding agents into your development workflow in a way that strengthens, instead of weakens, your codebase.

Key idea: treat agents as structured collaborators. That means:

🔸 Designing a clear workflow for how the agent proposes changes, and how humans review them.
🔸 Using tests as the contract between human developers and the agent.
🔸 Giving the agent enough context to be useful, without overwhelming it with every detail.

For the Dutch ecosystem, this matters. As organizations adopt AI tools at scale, responsible practices around code quality, traceability, and compliance become essential.

At KickstartAI, our mission is to accelerate AI adoption in a way that benefits both business and society. Strong engineering discipline around coding agents is a big part of that.

Want to see what this looks like in practice? Read the article here 👇
https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-best-practices-coding-agents-avoid-slop-increase-productivity

FS-Pre.article_title: Agentic Engineering I: A non-technical guide to working with coding agents
FS-Pre.post:
Many teams ask us: "Where do we even start with coding agents?"

Our latest guide offers a pragmatic entry point for organizations that want to experiment without losing control of quality, security, or scope.

Here are three concrete steps to get started:

🧪 Start with a contained pilot
Pick a narrow, repetitive task with low risk, such as refactoring, test generation, or internal tooling. Measure review time, defect rates, and developer satisfaction.

🧭 Redefine developer roles
Agree that the agent writes the first draft, and developers act as reviewers, architects, and risk owners. Make it explicit who signs off on changes.

📊 Build feedback loops
Use automated tests, code review checklists, and simple metrics to give agents structured feedback. Treat it as a continuous improvement cycle, not a one‑off experiment.

At KickstartAI, we believe that coding agents can significantly boost productivity in the Dutch ecosystem when teams combine experimentation with thoughtful governance.

Want to see what that looks like in practice? Check out the full non‑technical guide here:
https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-non-technical-guide-working-with-coding-agents

#AIAgents #EngineeringProductivity #DigitalInnovation #KickstartAI

FS-Pre.article_title: KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it
FS-Pre.post:
Many organizations in the Netherlands are asking the same question: how do you move from AI experiments to real impact at scale? ✨

KLM is a great example of what that journey can look like.

In a recent conversation, KLM CIO Aart Slagt shared how the airline is shifting from isolated pilots to a focused AI deployment strategy. The goal is clear: use AI where it adds real value in planning, optimization, and operations, while keeping human contact at the heart of the passenger experience.

That approach delivers tangible results. Projects like smarter catering predictions and more accurate contingency fuel planning show how AI can reduce waste, improve reliability, and support better decision-making across the business.

At KickstartAI, we see this as a blueprint for the Dutch ecosystem:

✅ Start with concrete operational challenges.
✅ Involve users early, so they understand and trust the models.
✅ Build the technical and organizational foundations to scale.

AI adoption is not about replacing people. It is about combining human expertise with data and models to create better outcomes for customers, employees, and society.

We are proud to see Dutch leaders like KLM take this step, and we are excited to help more organizations follow.

FS-Pre.article_title: Why managing GenAI projects requires a playbook tweak
FS-Pre.post:
How do you bring GenAI into a highly regulated domain like financial health, and still move fast? 🤝

In our recent collaboration with ING, KickstartAI helped build a GenAI assistant that supports people with complex financial questions. The challenge: create helpful, conversational answers that also respect strict rules, privacy requirements, and the spirit of the EU AI Act.

Our approach focused on:

⚖️ Aligning legal, risk, and product teams early, so “responsible” is built into the design, not added at the end.

🛟 Using guardrails, retrieval, and tool use to keep answers accurate, grounded, and within the assistant’s mandate.

📏 Setting up transparent evaluation methods, so stakeholders can see how the system behaves, and where it still needs work.

For us, this is what accelerating AI adoption in the Netherlands looks like: pairing technical innovation with trust, clarity, and long-term maintainability.

We are proud to work with partners who want GenAI systems that are not just impressive, but dependable and fair for users.

Read more about our joint lessons and practical patterns for responsible GenAI in finance. 👇

The success did not start with models. It started with:

NS shows how operational AI can improve efficiency and safety while staying aligned with European values on privacy and responsibility.

ZS-Post.article_title: Agentic Engineering II: Best practices to avoid slop and actually improve productivity with coding agents
ZS-Post.post:
🤖 Coding agents promise faster development cycles, but long‑term value comes from pairing that speed with strong engineering discipline.

Our new article on agentic engineering explores how teams can reach higher productivity without losing control of their codebase.

Key principles:

🔍 Maintain focus in coding sessions
Structured prompts, clear goals, and short iterative loops help the agent stay on track. Focused sessions reduce rework and make code reviews easier.

🧪 Put tests at the center
Test‑driven development gives coding agents a clear definition of “done.” When every change has a test, teams gain confidence in both functionality and future refactors.

🧭 Keep humans in the driver’s seat
Developers review, adapt, and integrate the agent’s work. The agent accelerates implementation; the team owns architecture, quality, and accountability.

For Dutch organizations operating under the EU AI Act, these habits support responsible AI use in critical development workflows.

At KickstartAI, we collaborate with partners across the Netherlands to turn practices like these into everyday reality, not just theory.

Read the full article to see how structured use of coding agents can upgrade your engineering culture. 🚀

#AIengineering #codingagents #EUAIAct #softwaredevelopment #KickstartAI

In many of the projects we see at KickstartAI, engineers spend less time typing code and more time:

• Designing robust architectures for agents to work within.
• Writing clear specifications, examples, and test cases.
• Reviewing, refactoring, and securing agent-generated code.
• Coordinating across disciplines to deliver real user value.

For Dutch organizations, this shift can unlock new levels of productivity, especially when combined with strong engineering culture and the guardrails of the EU AI Act.

Agentic AI fits perfectly with a learning mindset: teams iterate faster, experiment more safely, and focus on higher-level problems that matter for society.

At KickstartAI, we collaborate with partners across the Netherlands to turn these capabilities into practical tools for product teams, public institutions, and the wider ecosystem.

ZS-Post.article_title: KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it
ZS-Post.post:
✈️ Many organizations talk about moving from AI pilots to real impact. KLM is already doing it.

During a recent session, KLM CIO Aart Slagt shared how the airline shifted from experimentation to a clear AI deployment strategy. The focus is simple and powerful: use AI where it improves operations, and keep people where human contact matters most.

KLM now applies AI to planning and optimization, with concrete results:
• A catering prediction model that helps load the right meals, reducing waste and improving passenger experience.
• A contingency fuel model that supports smarter fueling decisions and more efficient operations.
• "Talia," a secure environment that lets employees work with AI tools safely.

Aart made one key point: an AI model only creates value when users understand it and trust it.

At KickstartAI, we see this mindset as essential for the Dutch ecosystem. Responsible deployment, transparent models, and secure environments help organizations scale AI with confidence.

Stories like KLM's show how the Netherlands can turn AI ambition into operational excellence and societal benefit.

🔗 Read the full story on our site.

ZS-Post.article_title: Why managing GenAI projects requires a playbook tweak
ZS-Post.post:
💡 GenAI products ask for a different playbook.

In our work with ING on a financial health assistant, we saw product, tech, and compliance teams face new questions: How do you scope a system that “talks back”? How do you measure quality when answers can vary, yet must stay safe and accurate?

Three principles stood out:

✅ Align expectations early.
Teams agree on what “a good answer” looks like, including guardrails, tone of voice, and how to handle uncertainty.

✅ Treat the model like a teammate.
Teams iterate with the assistant, review outputs together, and use real customer journeys to refine prompts, context, and UX.

GenAI in finance is high stakes, especially under the EU AI Act. With the right structures, it is also a powerful way to increase financial literacy and support customers when they need it most.

AI creates real value when it reaches the timetable, the platform, and the traveler.

At NS, Director of Data, Innovation and AI Daan Debie shared how their teams bring AI into daily operations. Think about predicting train crowdedness or estimating bike availability, so travelers make better choices in real time.

What stands out is how NS treats AI as a shared practice, not a side project:

🚆 Strong governance keeps AI aligned with safety, reliability, and public trust.
👩‍🏫 Comprehensive training prepares staff to work with new tools, not around them.
🧑‍🤝‍🧑 User involvement from day one ensures that solutions fit actual workflows.

European railways operate in a complex environment, and NS shows that organizations can still move fast when they design AI with governance and people in mind.

At KickstartAI, we love examples that turn AI into better journeys, smoother operations, and safer systems for everyone in the Netherlands.

Curious to learn more about NS’s approach and lessons learned? You can read the full story here:
https://kickstart.ai/news/daan-debie-ns-how-ns-brings-ai-from-pilot-to-practice

FS-Post.article_title: Agentic Engineering II: Best practices to avoid slop and actually improve productivity with coding agents
FS-Post.post:
Many teams want the speed of coding agents and the reliability of a senior engineer. With the right engineering approach, they can get closer to both. 🚀

In our new article, we look at coding agents as part of a broader agentic engineering pattern: systems that choose tools, act, and adapt based on outcomes. The key is not magic prompts, but solid engineering discipline.

We highlight practices that matter for responsible adoption in the Dutch ecosystem:

✅ Gradual context expansion
Teams define a small, testable task, let the agent work, then add more context only when needed.

✅ Clear quality gates
Tests, linters, and code review form a safety net around agent-generated code.

✅ Alignment with regulation
Strong controls and traceability make it easier to comply with the EU AI Act and sector rules.

At KickstartAI, we believe that structured, test-driven use of coding agents can raise the quality bar, not lower it. When organizations treat agents as governed collaborators, AI becomes a lever for sustainable innovation in Dutch industry, government, and society.

Read the full article for concrete patterns you can try in your next sprint. 💬

#ResponsibleAI #AIAgents #EUAIAct #KickstartAI #Netherlands

FS-Post.article_title: Agentic Engineering I: A non-technical guide to working with coding agents
FS-Post.post:
Coding agents do more than speed up development. They reshape what it means to be a developer. 🧩

In our latest article, we look at how agentic AI changes day-to-day work in software teams.

Developers increasingly:

🔍 Review and curate: Engineers evaluate agent proposals, spot edge cases, and guard architecture.
🧪 Design feedback loops: Teams set up tests, metrics, and monitoring so agents learn from results.
🧭 Steer scope: Developers and product owners decide which tasks are safe for agents and which require human creativity.

When organizations embrace this shift, they unlock real value:

✅ Senior engineers spend more time on system design and less on repetitive tasks.
✅ Junior talent learns faster by comparing their ideas with agent suggestions.
✅ Teams deliver features more quickly while keeping a human in charge of decisions.

At KickstartAI, we believe coding agents can strengthen the Dutch tech ecosystem when people stay at the center and AI amplifies their expertise.

Ready to reframe the role of your development team in an agentic world? Read the full guide to working with coding agents. 👇

https://kickstart.ai/news/agentic-engineering-non-technical-guide-working-with-coding-agents

#Developers #AIFutureOfWork #AIAgents #KickstartAI

FS-Post.article_title: KLM CIO Aart Slagt on why an AI model only has value if users understand and trust it
FS-Post.post:
KLM is entering a new phase with AI ✈️

CIO Aart Slagt describes a clear shift: move from many experiments to AI that lives in daily operations. That shift only works when people understand and trust the models they use.

At KLM, AI now supports planning, optimization, and catering predictions, and it reduces unnecessary fuel use. The value shows up in better service, less waste, and smoother operations.

The key ingredient is not only model performance. The key ingredient is user adoption.

✅ Teams explain how each model works in plain language.
✅ Users see the impact in their own workflow.
✅ KLM provides “Talia,” a secure environment for using AI tools safely.

KLM’s story reflects a broader movement in the Netherlands and across Europe: organizations want responsible, production-grade AI that supports people instead of replacing them.

At KickstartAI, we see the same pattern across our challenges. Successful AI adoption grows where:

• Employees trust the system.
• Governance is built in from day one.
• Benefits are concrete and measurable.

We are excited to see Dutch leaders like KLM show how to scale AI with both ambition and care.

🤝 Let’s keep building an AI-powered ecosystem where users understand, question, and embrace the models they work with.

#AIadoption #aviation #datainnovation #ResponsibleAI #KickstartAI

FS-Post.article_title: Why managing GenAI projects requires a playbook tweak
FS-Post.post:
GenAI in finance only creates lasting value when responsibility is built into the product, not bolted on at the end. 🛟

In our collaboration with ING on a financial health assistant, KickstartAI focused on practical ways to align innovation with the spirit of the EU AI Act and the Dutch financial context.

Three principles guided the work:

🔹 Clarity on financial advice boundaries
The assistant becomes safer when teams define which topics it can cover, how it phrases information, and where it must defer to regulated channels or human experts.

🔹 Traceability in design and evaluation
Trust grows when product choices, model behavior, and evaluation results remain transparent to both internal stakeholders and regulators.

🔹 Human oversight as a feature
Customers benefit when financial professionals can review, adjust, and improve AI‑generated interactions instead of operating in a black box.

These principles help organizations reach AI solutions that are both useful for customers and aligned with emerging regulations in Europe.

KickstartAI is committed to helping Dutch organizations turn responsible AI guidelines into working products that support financial well‑being at scale. 🤝

#ResponsibleAI #EUAIAct #FinTech #GenAI #KickstartAI

Phrases like 'Trust grows when product choices, model behavior, and evaluation results remain transparent' read as somewhat instructional and flat rather than genuinely human and upbeat.
